failure_surface: The generated Excel formula only returns a single date value instead of collecting and joining ALL matching dates from the referenced sheet for each material.
root_cause: The agent replaced the original array-based filtering pattern (IF(A2=ZORD!A:A,ZORD!C:C,"")) with an INDEX+MATCH construct that inherently returns only the first match, failing to capture multiple dates per material as required by the task.
minimal_fix_hypothesis: When aggregating data from another worksheet or range based on a matching condition, use array/filter patterns (e.g., FILTER function or IF array) rather than INDEX+MATCH lookups that return single values only.
evidence: The task instruction explicitly asks to 'join these various dates' implying multiple dates per cell (row 2 has three dates)
The original formula provided by the user uses IF(A2=ZORD!A:A,ZORD!C:C,'') which creates an array of ALL matching values
Agent's generated formula uses MATCH(TRUE,A2=ZORD!$A$2:$A$15,0) which returns position of FIRST match only
In the ZORD sheet, ABC1 appears 3 times with different dates - the solution must handle multiple matches
Verification showed formulas were written successfully but would only evaluate to single dates

memory_items.title: Array Filtering vs Single Lookup
memory_items.lesson: When retrieving multiple matching values from another worksheet for aggregation, prefer FILTER/IF array patterns over INDEX-MATCH which only returns single values.
memory_items.generalization: For tasks requiring collection of all matching records (TEXTJOIN, CONCATENATE, etc.), ensure the lookup method preserves row multiplicity. INDEX+MATCH collapses to first match; FILTER or IF-array preserves all matches.

memory_items.title: Date Formatting Within Array Functions
memory_items.lesson: When applying TEXT formatting to dates in array operations, wrap the entire array expression with TEXT() to format each element before joining.
memory_items.generalization: TEXT(array_expression, format) works when TEXT receives an array. Individual cell references inside TEXT JOIN require proper array construction to apply formatting uniformly.

memory_items.title: Cross-Sheet Data Aggregation Patterns
memory_items.lesson: Validate that formulas can actually produce expected output volume - if a cell should contain N joined values, the lookup pattern must extract N source values.
memory_items.generalization: Before finalizing cross-sheet aggregation formulas, verify the reference pattern supports the desired cardinality (single vs. multiple values) for each output cell.

edits.content:
### 7. Cross-Sheet Aggregation
- **Multiple Matches Required:** When collecting multiple values from another sheet (e.g., for TEXTJOIN, concatenation), use FILTER or IF-array patterns—not INDEX+MATCH—to preserve all matching rows.
- **Verify Output Cardinality:** Before finalizing aggregation formulas, confirm the lookup method produces the expected number of values (single vs. multiple) for each destination cell.
- **Array Formatting:** When formatting dates within arrays, apply TEXT() to the entire array expression, not individual cell references, to maintain uniform formatting across all joined values.
